name: GitHub
description: Talk to this agent by @mentioning it in GitHub issue and pull-request comments — a GitHub App with webhook signature verification via eve's githubChannel.
setup:
This channel is a **GitHub App** owned by the agent — its `@mention` identity in issues and pull requests, and (because an App installation is itself an API credential) the credential it uses to *work* the repositories you install it on. Each agent needs its own App: two agents sharing a slug can't both hear their mentions.

**Guided (recommended):** after installing this channel, open the agent's **Deployment** tab and click **Create GitHub App**. Eden registers the App on GitHub from a manifest (one confirmation screen — the name is editable there), stores all four secrets on this agent automatically, and sends you to GitHub to pick the repositories the App should watch. Zero copy-paste.

**Manual (fallback):**

1. Create a **GitHub App** (Settings → Developer settings → GitHub Apps). Note its **App ID** → `GITHUB_APP_ID` and its slug → `GITHUB_APP_SLUG` (the `@name` people mention).
2. Generate a **private key** (PEM) and put its full contents in `GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY`. Set a **webhook secret** → `GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET`.
3. Deploy the agent, then set the App's **Webhook URL** to `https://<your-domain>/e/<environmentId>/eve/v1/github` (shown on the Deployment tab).
4. Under the App's webhook events, subscribe to **Issue comment** and **Pull request review comment** (add `issues`, `pull_request`, or check/workflow events only if you wire up those hooks). Grant **Issues** and **Pull requests** read & write; add **Contents** read & write if the agent should clone, branch, and push.
5. Install the App on the repositories it should watch.

An issue or PR comment that `@mention`s the App's slug starts a turn.

secrets.name: GITHUB_APP_ID
secrets.description: The GitHub App's numeric App ID. Set automatically by Create GitHub App on the Deployment tab.

secrets.name: GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY
secrets.description: The GitHub App's private key (full PEM contents) — authenticates as the app to post replies and mint short-lived installation tokens for doing work. Set automatically by Create GitHub App.

secrets.name: GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET
secrets.description: The webhook secret configured on the App — eve verifies the inbound webhook signature against it. Set automatically by Create GitHub App.

secrets.name: GITHUB_APP_SLUG
secrets.description: The App's slug — the @name mentioned to trigger the agent. Set automatically by Create GitHub App (GitHub derives it from the final App name).
